records.ety: [L. induere to put on, clothe, fr. OL. indu (fr. in- in) + a root seen also in L. exuere to put off, divest, exuviae the skin of an animal, slough, induviae clothes. Cf. Endue to invest.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Indued (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Induing.]

defs: To put on, as clothes; to draw on.
quotes: The baron had indued a pair of jack boots.

defs: To clothe; to invest; hence, to endow; to furnish; to supply with moral or mental qualities.
quotes: Indu'd with robes of various hue she flies.
Indued with intellectual sense and souls.
